Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Megadev: A Development Kit for the Sega Mega Drive and Mega CD Hardware 30 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Baochip-1x: What It Is, Why I'm Doing It Now and How It Came About 66 upvotes, 8 comments
  3. Can I run AI locally? 1260 upvotes, 311 comments
  4. How Lego builds a new Lego set 17 upvotes, 1 comments
  5. Show HN: Channel Surfer – Watch YouTube like it’s cable TV 532 upvotes, 157 comments
  6. Mouser: An open source alternative to Logi-Plus mouse software 340 upvotes, 101 comments
  7. I found 39 Algolia admin keys exposed across open source documentation sites 137 upvotes, 36 comments
  8. XML Is a Cheap DSL 35 upvotes, 6 comments
  9. The Isolation Trap: Erlang 74 upvotes, 20 comments
  10. 1M context is now generally available for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 828 upvotes, 323 comments
  11. Hammerspoon 299 upvotes, 107 comments
  12. You gotta think outside the hypercube 80 upvotes, 23 comments
  13. Atari 2600 BASIC Programming (2015) 35 upvotes, 8 comments
  14. Michael Faraday: Scientist and Nonconformist (1996) 11 upvotes, 1 comments
  15. Optimizing Content for Agents 52 upvotes, 21 comments
  16. Games with loot boxes to get minimum 16 age rating across Europe 234 upvotes, 137 comments
  17. AEP (API Design Standard and Tooling Ecosystem) 22 upvotes, 7 comments
  18. Recursive Problems Benefit from Recursive Solutions 29 upvotes, 11 comments
  19. A Survival Guide to a PhD (2016) 124 upvotes, 71 comments
  20. New 'negative light' technology hides data transfers in plain sight 93 upvotes, 55 comments
  21. Emacs and Vim in the Age of AI 167 upvotes, 112 comments
  22. Python: The Optimization Ladder 7 upvotes, 1 comments
  23. Qatar helium shutdown puts chip supply chain on a two-week clock 626 upvotes, 528 comments
  24. RAM kits are now sold with one fake RAM stick alongside a real one 35 upvotes, 20 comments
  25. Digg is gone again 229 upvotes, 211 comments
  26. Claude Code conducts A/B tests on core features 95 upvotes, 79 comments
  27. Parallels confirms MacBook Neo can run Windows in a virtual machine 281 upvotes, 389 comments
  28. Coding after coders: The end of computer programming as we know it? 143 upvotes, 188 comments
  29. I beg you to follow Crocker's Rules, even if you will be rude to me 87 upvotes, 126 comments
  30. Wired headphone sales are exploding 192 upvotes, 313 comments

Last generated 9:00pm, 14 March 2026. Source here.
